name: Clarence Williams
description: American jazz pianist, composer, promoter, vocalist, theatrical producer, and publisher (1893-1965)
abstract: Clarence Williams (October 8, 1898 or October 6, 1893 – November 6, 1965) was an American jazz pianist, composer, promoter, vocalist, theatrical producer, and publisher.

about_quotes.speaker: George Justin
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee does not mention Clarence Williams. The interviewer mentions knowing Clarence Williams, but the interviewee's response ('Well, there was another group...') does not acknowledge or confirm any personal connection to Clarence Williams.

about_quotes.speaker: John "Knocky" Parker
about_quotes.evidence: We had, I remember, things by Kalkia Davenport and old Jelly Roll, lots of Jelly Roll Martin pieces and Clarence Williams, early Wildflower Rag and Argan Grindr Blues

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: "when Clarence Williams and Perry Bradford, who were really the pioneers, they were the pioneers in starting this branch of ethnic recording."

about_quotes.speaker: Jasper Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: I met, uh, Piran, the violinist, and Clarence William, the pianist.

about_quotes.speaker: Dominic James "Nick" La Rocca
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions 'Clarence Williams' only as a pianist in a list and as the leader of the 'Clarence Williams' Blue Five' which featured Louis Armstrong.
